journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan - old note replaced by new note
journal_entry.rationale: The refinancing transaction extinguishes the old loan liability (debit to decrease liability d=1) and establishes a new loan liability (credit to increase liability a=1). This is a pure liability swap with no cash movement, no gain/loss, and no tax implications. The old note payable of $100,000 is removed from the books and the new note payable of $100,000 is recorded.

output.reason: Refinancing replaces old loan with new loan. The debit side removes the old liability by debiting (decreasing) the old loan payable = 1 liability decrease. The credit side will record the new loan (liability increase), but we only classify debits here.

debit_classifier.reason: Refinancing replaces old loan with new loan. The debit side removes the old liability by debiting (decreasing) the old loan payable = 1 liability decrease. The credit side will record the new loan (liability increase), but we only classify debits here.

output.reason: Refinancing involves replacing an old loan with a new loan. On the credit side: (1) the old loan liability is removed/decreased, which requires a debit to the old loan liability account, and (2) the new loan liability is created/increased, which requires a credit. The credit side shows only the new loan liability increase = 1 liability increase line.

credit_classifier.reason: Refinancing involves replacing an old loan with a new loan. The credit side shows only the new loan liability increase = 1 liability increase line.
